response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned senior software developer with over 15 years of experience, but he is a near-total mismatch for this embedded networking security role. The most critical disqualifier is the complete absence of Japanese language proficiency, which is an explicit hard requirement. Beyond that, his technical background is overwhelmingly web and cloud-centric — TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB — with only incidental C/C++ exposure and no demonstrable experience in embedded firmware, Layer-2/3 networking, LAN switch hardware, or any of the required security domains such as MACsec, 802.1X, or captive portal systems.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience spanning web, platform, embedded systems, and systems-level programming
Demonstrated C/C++ exposure through National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI) and a wave field synthesis thesis project using Linux and Jack audio
Strong engineering discipline — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and craftsmanship practices that support quality-focused product development
concerns: Zero Japanese language proficiency — an explicitly stated hard requirement that alone disqualifies the candidate
No experience with embedded networking, LAN switch products, or any of the listed security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, MAC authentication, device attack detection)
No evidence of Linux kernel investigation, source-code modification, or chip-vendor SDK usage; all Linux exposure appears to be at the user-space application level

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security domain expertise
Broad engineering experience with some C/C++ and Linux exposure
